accepted_explanation: Repository restructuring (langchain-community moving to a separate repo) combined with the release of langchain-core v0.3.56 increased review complexity and slowed PR merges during this window.
limitation_notes: Inline code review comments (separate from discussion comments) were not fetched. Reviewer availability data not in the dataset.

accepted_explanation: A batch of 'Add ruff rules' PRs opened in January 2025 were merged together in a coordinated backlog cleanup event (March 31 – April 4). The median was elevated by these long-lived PRs merging in a cluster.
limitation_notes: Codex confirmed this is a backlog-cleanup artifact, not genuine review slowdown. The median is skewed by coordinated old-PR merges.

accepted_explanation: Mix of stale PRs being merged alongside normal activity. Median elevated by a small number of long-lived PRs merging during a moderate-volume week.
limitation_notes: Expected evidence URLs not pre-identified for this window — citation_coverage will not be scored.

accepted_explanation: Moderate anomaly. Weaker signal than adjacent windows — likely a combination of normal variation and a few older PRs merging.
Early signal of the stale-PR backlog pattern. Median elevated by a small number of long-lived PRs. Weaker signal than the April windows.

A single PR (#9839) that had been open for approximately 373 days was merged during a quiet week (only 3 PRs merged total). This one stale PR drove ~99.7% of the average merge time. Not a genuine health signal — a cleanup artifact.
limitation_notes: This is a known data artifact. The correct detector metric (median) would suppress this. Agent should identify the single outlier PR as the root cause.

accepted_explanation: Similar stale-PR artifact pattern. One or two long-lived PRs merged during a low-volume week. Low activity repo makes these signals noisy.

A batch of long-lived PRs (some open since November 2024) were merged during this window alongside normal volume. The median was elevated but the typical PR was still merging within ~25 hours — this is a mixed backlog-clearing signal rather than a repo-wide slowdown.
limitation_notes: Codex identified PR #10546 as the main outlier (3483h, open since Nov 2024). Typical PRs were healthy.

accepted_explanation: PR #28972 (open since Dec 30, 2024, merged Mar 17) was a major outlier in the mean-based metric. With median, this window shows a weaker signal. Demonstrates the importance of using median over mean for this metric.
limitation_notes: This case uses the old mean-based metric for historical comparison. The median-based detector correctly scores this as z=3.4 (weaker signal).

accepted_explanation: Duplicate of dbt_pr_slowdown_2025_04_12. Included to validate that the agent consistently identifies PR #9839 as the root cause across separate runs.
limitation_notes: Consistency check — same window as dbt_pr_slowdown_2025_04_12.
